ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensor_data" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sample_number" INTEGER /* Sequential number of the sample collected */,
  "period_seconds" REAL /* Period of the sample collection in seconds */,
  "gravity_m_per_s2" REAL /* Gravity measurement in meters per second squared */,
  "velocity_m_per_s" REAL /* Velocity measurement in meters per second */,
  "temperature_celsius" REAL /* Temperature measurement in degrees Celsius */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the sample was collected */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the machine from which the data was collected */,
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of sensor */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the sensor data */,
  "anomaly_flag" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the data point is considered an anomaly */,
  "alert_generated" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if an alert was generated based on this data point */,
  "data_quality_score" INTEGER /* Score to indicate the quality of the data (0-100) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., real-time, batch, simulation) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id", "sample_number"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_sensor_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_type_id") REFERENCES sensor_types ("sensor_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machines" (
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine */,
  "machine_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the machine in the production line */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the machine (e.g., operational, maintenance, offline) */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance performed on the machine */,
  "sensor_ids" TEXT /* Array of sensor IDs associated with the machine */,
  "machine_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of machine */,
  "current_operator_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the current operator of the machine */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date for the next maintenance */,
  "maintenance_history_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the maintenance history of the machine */,
  "production_rate" REAL /* Current production rate of the machine */,
  "last_alert_date" TEXT /* Date of the last alert generated for the machine */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_machines_maintenance_history_id FOREIGN KEY ("maintenance_history_id") REFERENCES maintenance_history ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_machines_current_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("current_operator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_machines_machine_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_type_id") REFERENCES machine_types ("machine_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_types" (
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor type */,
  "sensor_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor type */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Default unit of measurement for the sensor type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machine_types" (
  "machine_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine type */,
  "machine_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the machine type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_history" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the machine */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance performed */,
  "details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the maintenance performed */,
  "performed_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who performed the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_history_performed_by FOREIGN KEY ("performed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
